prompt: I want to set up this KMP project as a new app called "TaskFlow" — a task management app targeting Android and iOS. Use Kotlin Multiplatform with Compose Multiplatform, Room for database, Ktor for networking, Koin for DI, and Jetpack Navigation 3 for navigation. Please: 1) Create project management files in a .skills/ directory following the blueprint pattern (a guide with phased execution, hints for KMP best practices, and a validation plan), 2) Create an AGENTS.md with project-specific instructions, 3) Set up the Gradle dependencies in libs.versions.toml, 4) Set up basic navigation with 4 screens: Home, Tasks, Settings, and TaskDetail. Only target Android and iOS — remove any desktop/web targets if present.
source: Greenfield KMP project scaffolding from kmm-basic-sample template
agent_rubric: An AGENTS.md file exists at the project root with project-specific instructions for AI agents working on this KMP project
A .skills/ directory is created with at least a guide task file (.skills/kmp-baseline-guide-task/SKILL.md) that has phased execution steps
The guide task uses the correct task prefixes: **User Action**, **Agent Action**, and **Validation** for different task types
The guide task uses Markdown checkboxes (- [ ]) for tracking task completion state
gradle/libs.versions.toml includes Room, Ktor, and Koin dependencies with specific version numbers
Navigation uses Jetpack Navigation 3 (org.jetbrains.androidx.navigation3), NOT the older navigation-compose library
Desktop and web targets (jvm, wasmJs, js) are removed from shared/build.gradle.kts — only Android and iOS remain
Basic navigation screens are set up (Home, Tasks, Settings, TaskDetail) with navigation between them
The shared module's commonMain source set contains the navigation setup code
The project structure maintains the standard KMP layout with shared/, androidApp/ or composeApp/, and iosApp/
